skill.name: documentation-generator
skill.description: Generate JSDoc, docstrings, README sections, and API documentation from existing source code.
skill.summary: Creates clear developer documentation for functions, classes, modules, APIs, and project README files.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives from fenced documentation examples, README template text, and normal prose. No executable scripts, network behavior, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Examples Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown examples for JSDoc, Python docstrings, usage syntax, and README templates. They do not define executable skill logic and do not pass user input to a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious text is inside Markdown code fences or example command snippets. There is no script file, dynamic command construction, or execution instruction in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Ordinary Documentation Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain the skill description, documentation checklist items, placeholder README text, or final guidance. No hashing, encryption, password handling, or cryptographic algorithm use appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection found no cryptographic API, algorithm name used for security, or credential-processing context. The matches are prose in a documentation guide.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Prose Flagged as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged line advises writers to avoid jargon. It does not request host details, environment variables, usernames, process lists, network data, or other reconnaissance output.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a documentation style guideline with no command, tool call, or data collection request. The semantic context clearly dismisses reconnaissance risk.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when code behavior is not documented. This skill creates concise docs, examples, and README content from existing code.
actual_capabilities: Drafts JSDoc comments for JavaScript and TypeScript functions.
Creates Python docstrings in Google, NumPy, or Sphinx style.
Outlines README files with overview, installation, usage, API, and license sections.
Documents functions, classes, modules, parameters, returns, errors, and side effects.
Adapts documentation style to existing project conventions.
limitations: It depends on readable source code and clear project structure.
It may need user review for business rules and domain meaning.
It does not publish docs to hosting services by itself.
It should not replace maintainers for legal, license, or security statements.

use_cases.title: Fill Missing Function Comments
use_cases.description: Generate concise comments for undocumented functions, including parameters, return values, examples, and possible errors.

use_cases.title: Prepare Project README Content
use_cases.description: Create a README outline with installation, usage, configuration, API reference, contributing, and license sections.

use_cases.title: Standardize API Documentation
use_cases.description: Convert mixed documentation into a consistent format that follows project conventions and language-specific standards.

prompt_templates.title: Document One Function
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small code unit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use documentation-generator to write a clear docstring for this function. Include purpose, parameters, return value, errors, and one simple example.

prompt_templates.title: Document a Source Folder
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for several related files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use documentation-generator on the source files in this folder. Add missing public API documentation and match the existing project style.

prompt_templates.title: Create README Sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Use documentation-generator to draft README sections for overview, installation, usage, configuration, API reference, contributing, and license.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Improve API Docs
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for documentation quality review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use documentation-generator to review current API documentation for gaps, stale examples, missing errors, and unclear parameter descriptions. Then propose improved text.

output_examples.input: A JavaScript utility function with parameters for price, tax, and discount.
output_examples.output: A JSDoc block that describes the calculation, each numeric parameter, the returned total, error cases, and a practical usage example.

output_examples.input: A Python service class with public methods but no docstrings.
output_examples.output: Google-style docstrings for the class and methods, including responsibilities, arguments, return values, raised errors, and side effects.

output_examples.input: A small library project without a README.
output_examples.output: A README draft with project overview, feature list, installation steps, basic usage, API reference, configuration notes, and license section.

best_practices: Provide enough source context for accurate parameter, return, and error descriptions.
Ask the skill to match the style already used in the repository.
Review generated examples against real behavior before publishing documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not generate docs from partial code when important types or dependencies are missing.
Do not overwrite carefully written custom documentation without review.
Do not accept generated legal, security, or license wording without maintainer approval.

faq.question: What languages does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It describes support for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Rust, Java, and Go documentation styles.

faq.question: Can it generate a full README?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft README sections for overview, installation, usage, API reference, configuration, contributing, and license.

faq.question: Does it execute project code?
faq.answer: No evidence found that the skill executes project code. It is a Markdown instruction file for documentation generation.

faq.question: Can it match an existing documentation style?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the assistant to match existing documentation style in the project.

faq.question: Should generated documentation be reviewed?
faq.answer: Yes. Maintainers should verify examples, edge cases, errors, and domain-specific behavior before publishing.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only false positives in static analysis and no malicious behavior.
